mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production directly raises manufacturing profitability and aggregate output, which translates into higher expected corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank information coefficient between INDPRO change and S&P 500 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8 percent reflects substantial labor market slack that suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in consumer prices alongside a stable or decreasing federal funds rate compresses real interest rates, stimulates borrowing and risk-taking, and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A pooled regression of S&P 500 20-day returns on the interaction term between CPIAUCSL change and FEDFUNDS level yields a t-statistic below 1.5 or negative coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, increases in the federal funds rate raise discount rates and corporate debt service costs, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative during tightening cycles as defined by a rising FEDFUNDS trend, with a one-sided t-test p-value exceeding 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength and construction activity, which lifts aggregate corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS change and HOUST change exhibits a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below an index level of 95 indicates a contraction in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which reduces earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 45 percent of triggered months when INDPRO is below 95.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls indicates deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income and consumption forecasts, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of months following a PAYEMS decline, failing a binomial test against a 50 percent baseline at the 5 percent significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP during a post-recession expansion reflects recovering aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55 percent of quarters when GDPC1 increases, or the Rank IC between GDPC1 change and forward returns falls below 0.02.
